COT Composite Backtest — every report, every trade, 2022 → 2026

Mechanical Positioning + Flow signal from 235 weekly CFTC Disaggregated reports, traded only after each report's verified public release (holiday-shifted calendar; ransomware/shutdown backlog weeks excluded), priced on front-month continuous OHLC with lumber roll gaps neutralized. Gross of costs, sign-only sizing.

OverviewEquityMarkets Bracket labReport browserWeek recapTrade log Method

01Overview

02Equity — cumulative return per $100k leg

Sum of weekly returns (not compounded). Portfolio = equal-notional mean of active executable legs each report week. Steel-HRC is dealer-assessed cash-settled — scored but never traded (§11.7). Click chips to toggle; hover for the crosshair.

03Per-market outcome

Cumulative adjusted return over all traded weeks. Blue = net positive, red = net negative (diverging by sign).

04Bracket lab — fixed take-profit × stop-loss × time stop

Every executable trade re-simulated with a fixed TP/SL bracket and a time stop, walking daily bars from entry (§7 rules; same-bar TP+SL resolves stop-first — conservative). Pick a hold below; each cell = average return per trade at that TP × SL. Click a cell to load its detail. The weekly-roll baseline (no bracket) is shown for comparison.

R:R view — same brackets, organized by reward-to-risk

Every TP×SL pair expressed as a reward:risk ratio, at the time stop chosen above, for the market selected above. Entry is always the first session open after release. Exp (R) = average return per trade divided by the risk (SL%) — expectancy in R-multiples; positive means the bracket made more than one risk unit per trade on average. Click a row to load it everywhere.

R:RTP %SL %TP hitSL hitTime exit Win rateAvg ret/tradeExp (R)Total ret

R sweep — every ratio from 0.5 : 1 to 10 : 1, step 0.2

Continuous reward:risk curve: for each stop size, take-profit swept as R × SL across 48 ratios. Reconstructed exactly from each trade's max-favorable-excursion-before-stop (verified bit-for-bit against the simulated brackets at build time). Y = total return across all trades in the pool above; hover for per-ratio detail. Same time stop and market filter as the lab.

05Report browser — what each report said

Every weekly report, factor by factor: managed-money crowding → Positioning, commercial vs retail flow → Flow, the composite lean, and the trade that followed at the first legal print after release. Weeks with a verified factor-override file (price-evidence Trend/Tape + judgment Flow, e.g. 2026-06-23 and 2026-06-30) show the Published weights first; the backtest always traded the mechanical signal. TP/SL levels and bracket outcome follow the selection made in the lab above.

Full universe — every market in this report

All markets in the weekly CFTC Disaggregated file (energy, ags, softs, power, gas basis, env credits…), scored by the same mechanical rules. Sorted most-short → most-long. ENV = structurally excluded env-credit market (§5); MM0 = zero managed-money book; FLOW-NA = market absent from prior report. Published column appears on override weeks.

MarketOIMM %OIComm Δ% PFCompLean (L/S)Published (L/S)Flags

07Trade log — every executable trade

08Method & integrity notes

Research artifact, not financial advice. The composite ranks evidence; it does not size positions (methodology §0). All results gross of commissions and spread (§11.6). n-discipline applies: no edge conclusions at this sample (§7.6).
